steps.message:
<uploaded_files>/app</uploaded_files>

Subject: escalation - recurring CRM data-quality complaints from the field

I'm writing this up on behalf of Customer Success. Over the past few sprints our
account managers have logged a run of small but genuine mistakes in how the contact
tooling saves and shows two fields: a contact's mobile number and their loan type.
Every one of these has been reproduced on a live account, so please treat them as
real defects rather than user error. They only bite when a contact's details come in
a slightly off-pattern shape, which is why they slipped past everyone for weeks - the
routine cases (a plain US mobile, a loan type chosen from the dropdown) come out
perfectly correct, and our automated checks only ever exercise those routine cases.

Here is what the managers actually reported, in their words:

- "I onboarded a borrower in London and typed their mobile the way it's printed on
  their letterhead. Our workspace is configured to default to the UK, but the number
  saved under a US code (+1...) instead of the UK code (+44...)." So: when the digits
  could belong to more than one country, the contact's configured home region is being
  ignored and the wrong country is winning.

- "A few contacts gave me their number in the international style that starts with a
  double-zero access code - a 0044 UK number, for instance. Instead of accepting it,
  the tool told me the number was invalid and I had to retype it by hand." So: the 00
  international prefix isn't being treated as equivalent to a leading +.

- "One of my older accounts is tagged with a loan type from before we cleaned up the
  catalog, and the label on the record printed as 'Hardmoney' - no space - when it
  should read 'Hard Money', same word-by-word capitalization as every other loan type
  we display." So: an unfamiliar loan-type code isn't being turned into a readable,
  properly spaced and capitalized label.

- "A rep entered a loan type as 'Working_Capital', with the odd capitals and the
  underscore, and that's exactly the string that got stored on the contact - not the
  tidy internal code the rest of the system matches against." So: an oddly-cased entry
  of a known type is being stored verbatim rather than normalized to its standard code.

There may be more corners like these; those are simply the ones we could reproduce.
The common thread is that all of it is pure string-and-lookup work - deciding a
country for a number, reshaping a dialed number into storage form, and mapping a
loan-type string to its code and its display label. No database or network calls are
involved, so each case is reproducible from a direct call.

What I need from engineering: make the saved and displayed values correct for these
unusual-but-legitimate inputs, and do it without disturbing how the ordinary inputs
(the ones our current checks cover) already behave. Please don't touch the existing
test files, and confirm the current suite still passes when you're done:

    cd /app/loangen-agent && python -m pytest tests -v
I'll investigate this codebase to understand the contact tooling and the reported defects.

arguments.prompt:
Explore the codebase at /app/loangen-agent. I'm looking for code that handles two things for a "contact":
1. Phone/mobile number parsing, validation, and storage formatting (likely involves country/region defaults, international dialing prefixes like + and 00).
2. Loan type normalization - mapping a loan-type string to an internal code and to a display label.

Please find and report:
- The exact file paths and relevant functions/classes handling phone number processing.
- The exact file paths and relevant functions/classes handling loan type code/label mapping.
- The test files (under tests/) that cover these.
- Any config for a contact's home region/country default.
- Show me the full relevant source code snippets with file paths and line numbers.

Be thorough - "medium" thoroughness. Report code contents so I can make edits.

results.content:
<task id="ses_09b2d907affe2Smm1Qsknl2lLB" state="completed">
<task_result>
I now have a complete picture. Here is my report.

---

# Search Report: Phone Processing & Loan Type Normalization for "Contact"

## 1. Phone Number Processing

### Primary module — the actual parsing/validation/formatting logic
**`/app/loangen-agent/agent/integrations/cartesia/phone.py`** (154 lines)

This is where all the real work happens, using the `phonenumbers` library. It handles international prefixes (`+` and `00`), a default region, and multi-region fallback.

Key constants and functions:

```python
16: _NON_DIGIT_RE = re.compile(r"[^\d+]+")
17: _FALLBACK_REGION = "US"
18: # Regions tried when no + country code is provided (default region first).
19: _CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS = ("US", "CA", "IN", "GB", "AU", "MX", "DE", "FR")
```

- `_format_parsed_number(parsed, *, require_valid)` — lines 22-28: formats to E.164, gated by `is_valid_number` / `is_possible_number`.
- `_try_parse_candidate(candidate, region, *, require_valid)` — lines 31-41: wraps `phonenumbers.parse`, swallows `NumberParseException`.
- **`try_normalize_phone_to_e164(raw, *, default_region=None)`** — lines 44-105: best-effort (returns `Optional[str]`, never raises). This is the one to edit for CRM import behavior.
  - Line 60-61: converts a leading `00` to `+` (`"+" + value[2:]`).
  - Line 67-72: explicit `+` handling.
  - Line 74-97: region fallback loop, preferring valid over possible matches.
  - Line 99-104: digit-count fallbacks (10 digits → `+1`; 11 starting with `1` → `+`; 8-15 digits → `+digits`).

    Note a subtle asymmetry worth reviewing: in `try_normalize_...` (line 76-78) the default region is only appended to `regions` when `not default` (i.e., when it's empty) — a likely bug, since a truthy default region is never explicitly prepended (it only appears if also in `_CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS`).

- **`normalize_phone_to_e164(raw, *, default_region=None)`** — lines 108-154: strict version that **raises `ValueError`** on invalid/impossible numbers.
  - Line 124-125: `00` prefix handling — note this uses `"+" + value[1:]` (keeps one leading `0`), which differs from the `try_` variant's `value[2:]`. Another asymmetry to review.

### Contact-layer wrappers (SMB CRM)
**`/app/loangen-agent/agent/services/smbcontacts/service.py`**

```python
21: from agent.integrations.cartesia.phone import (
22:     normalize_phone_to_e164,
23:     try_normalize_phone_to_e164,
24: )
...
89: def _default_phone_region() -> str:
90:     return settings.cartesia_default_phone_region.strip() or "US"
93: def normalize_mobile_for_storage(raw: str) -> str:
95:     return normalize_phone_to_e164(raw, default_region=_default_phone_region())
98: def try_normalize_mobile(raw: str) -> Optional[str]:
99:     return try_normalize_phone_to_e164(raw, default_region=_default_phone_region())
```

Other contact-facing helpers in the same file:
- `_mobile_from_input_or_error(mobile_raw)` — lines 102-119: for create/update (PATCH); empty string clears, invalid raises `HTTPException 400 INVALID_MOBILE_NUMBER`.
- `_resolve_mobile_for_import(mobile_raw, *, row_num, email)` — lines 122-158: CSV import; produces `CSVRowError` warnings instead of hard failures.
- `_normalize_contact_phone(contact)` — lines 569-584: normalizes a stored contact's mobile just before dialing.

### Other consumers of the region default
- `/app/loangen-agent/agent/services/calls/service.py:1786` — `default_region=settings.cartesia_default_phone_region.strip() or "US"`
- `/app/loangen-agent/agent/services/calls/inbound.py:193` — `default_region = settings.cartesia_default_phone_region.strip() or "US"`

---

## 2. Loan Type Normalization (code + label mapping)

### Primary module for CRM contacts / invites
**`/app/loangen-agent/agent/services/smbcontacts/loan_types.py`** (119 lines) — this is the canonical mapping of loan-type string → internal `id` and display `label`.

```python
12: LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES: List[dict[str, str]] = [
13:     {"id": "sba7a", "label": "SBA 7(a) Loan"},
14:     {"id": "sba504", "label": "SBA 504 Loan"},
...
33: ]
35: _LOAN_TYPE_IDS = {c["id"] for c in LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES}
38: _EXTRA_ALIASES: dict[str, str] = { "sba 7a": "sba7a", ... "usda b&i": "usda" }
```

Functions:
- **`loan_type_label(loan_type_id)`** — lines 88-94: id → display label (fallback `id.replace("_","").title()`).
- **`resolve_loan_type(raw)`** — lines 97-115: any string → canonical id (or `None`). Tries compact-match against ids (line 102-104), exact-lower id (105-106), normalized alias (`_normalize_key`, 107-109), then label/id normalized comparison (110-114).
- **`is_valid_loan_type_id(loan_type_id)`** — lines 118-119.
- Helper `_normalize_key(value)` — lines 84-85: lowercases, collapses `_`/`-` to spaces.

Consumers:
- `smbcontacts/service.py:35` imports `resolve_loan_type`; used in `_parse_loan_type_from_csv` (lines 161-184).
- `smbinvites/schemas.py:5,21` — `resolve_loan_type` used in `validate_loan_type_id` field validator.
- `smbinvites/service.py:35` imports `LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES, loan_type_label` (used for invite conversation confirm step, lines 442-461, 1213-1235).

### Separate, distinct loan-type label mapping (CRE qualification — NOT the CRM contact one)
**`/app/loangen-agent/agent/services/cre_qualification/lender_match.py`** — an independent, smaller label map used for lender/product matching (not contact storage):

```python
20: _LOAN_TYPE_LABELS: Dict[str, str] = {
21:     "term": "Term Loan",
22:     "sba7a": "SBA 7(a) Loan",
...
31: }
46: def format_application_loan_type(loan_type: str) -> str:   # lines 46-56
67: def product_matches_application_loan_type(...) -> Tuple[bool, str]:  # lines 67+
```

If your edit concerns a **contact's** loan type, the relevant file is `smbcontacts/loan_types.py`. `lender_match.py` is a parallel mapping for the CRE qualification/lender-matching flow — flagging it in case both need to stay in sync.

---

## 3. Config for Home Region / Country Default

**`/app/loangen-agent/agent/core/config.py`**

```python
246:     # Optional ISO region for CRM mobiles stored without + (e.g. US, IN, GB).
247:     # Defaults to US so numbers like (512) 555-0142 work without a country code.
248:     cartesia_default_phone_region: str = "US"
...
319:     @field_validator("cartesia_default_phone_region", mode="before")
320:     @classmethod
321:     def default_cartesia_phone_region(cls, v: object) -> object:
322:         if v is None or (isinstance(v, str) and not v.strip()):
323:             return "US"
324:         return v
```

Env var name: `CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION` (documented in `.env.example`). Accessed as `settings.cartesia_default_phone_region`.

---

## 4. Test Files

### Main test covering BOTH phone normalization AND loan types for contacts
**`/app/loangen-agent/tests/test_smb_contacts_csv_and_update.py`** (331 lines)
- `LoanTypeResolverTests` (lines 16-27): `resolve_loan_type` canonical id / label / alias / unknown.
- `PhoneNormalizationTests` (lines 30-61): US number, Indian number without country code (`"8050306043"` → `"+918050306043"`), explicit international, invalid → None. Patches `smb_contact_service.settings.cartesia_default_phone_region`.
- `ImportContactsCsvTests` (line 64+): CSV import including `loan_type`/`mobile` columns and warnings.
- Update/PATCH tests around lines 231-257 (international mobile update) and 291-324.

### Related tests
- **`/app/loangen-agent/tests/test_smb_invite_schemas.py`** (lines 13-34): `loan_type_id` validation via schema (`sba7a` accepted, unknown rejected, omitted → None).
- **`/app/loangen-agent/tests/test_smb_invite_conversation.py`** (lines 26-50): `crm_hint_loan_type` propagation.
- **`/app/loangen-agent/tests/test_lender_product_match.py`** (lines 1-43): tests `format_application_loan_type` and `product_matches_application_loan_type` from `cre_qualification/lender_match.py`.
- **`/app/loangen-agent/tests/test_bridge_broadway_qualification.py`** (line 11+): also exercises `product_matches_application_loan_type`.
- **`/app/loangen-agent/tests/test_inbound_calls.py`** (lines 180-266): patches `agent.services.calls.service.normalize_phone_to_e164` and sets `cartesia_default_phone_region`.

---

## Summary of files you'll most likely edit

| Concern | File | Key symbols |
|---|---|---|
| Phone parse/validate/format | `agent/integrations/cartesia/phone.py` | `try_normalize_phone_to_e164` (44), `normalize_phone_to_e164` (108), `_CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS` (19), `00`-prefix handling (60, 124) |
| Phone contact wrappers | `agent/services/smbcontacts/service.py` | `_default_phone_region` (89), `normalize_mobile_for_storage` (93), `try_normalize_mobile` (98) |
| Loan type id/label mapping (contacts) | `agent/services/smbcontacts/loan_types.py` | `LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES` (12), `resolve_loan_type` (97), `loan_type_label` (88) |
| Loan type label (CRE/lender — parallel) | `agent/services/cre_qualification/lender_match.py` | `_LOAN_TYPE_LABELS` (20), `format_application_loan_type` (46) |
| Region/country default config | `agent/core/config.py` | `cartesia_default_phone_region` (248), validator (319) |
| Tests (phone + loan type) | `tests/test_smb_contacts_csv_and_update.py` | `PhoneNormalizationTests`, `LoanTypeResolverTests` |

Two asymmetries worth noting before editing `phone.py`: (a) the `00`→`+` slicing differs between the two functions (`value[2:]` vs `value[1:]`, lines 61 vs 125), and (b) `try_normalize_phone_to_e164` only appends the default region to the fallback list when it is empty (lines 76-78), so a configured non-US default may not actually be tried first.
</task_result>
</task>

steps.message: Let me read the key files in detail.

results.content:
<path>/app/loangen-agent/agent/integrations/cartesia/phone.py</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: """
2: E.164 phone normalization for Cartesia outbound calls.
3: 
4: Numbers may be stored with or without a + country code. When omitted, the number
5: is parsed using CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION (default: US).
6: """
7: 
8: from __future__ import annotations
9: 
10: import re
11: from typing import Optional
12: 
13: import phonenumbers
14: from phonenumbers import NumberParseException, PhoneNumberFormat
15: 
16: _NON_DIGIT_RE = re.compile(r"[^\d+]+")
17: _FALLBACK_REGION = "US"
18: # Regions tried when no + country code is provided (default region first).
19: _CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS = ("US", "CA", "IN", "GB", "AU", "MX", "DE", "FR")
20: 
21: 
22: def _format_parsed_number(parsed: phonenumbers.PhoneNumber, *, require_valid: bool) -> Optional[str]:
23:     if require_valid:
24:         if not phonenumbers.is_valid_number(parsed):
25:             return None
26:     elif not phonenumbers.is_possible_number(parsed):
27:         return None
28:     return phonenumbers.format_number(parsed, PhoneNumberFormat.E164)
29: 
30: 
31: def _try_parse_candidate(
32:     candidate: str,
33:     region: Optional[str],
34:     *,
35:     require_valid: bool,
36: ) -> Optional[str]:
37:     try:
38:         parsed = phonenumbers.parse(candidate, region)
39:     except NumberParseException:
40:         return None
41:     return _format_parsed_number(parsed, require_valid=require_valid)
42: 
43: 
44: def try_normalize_phone_to_e164(
45:     raw: str,
46:     *,
47:     default_region: Optional[str] = None,
48: ) -> Optional[str]:
49:     """
50:     Best-effort E.164 normalization for CRM storage.
51: 
52:     Accepts international numbers with or without a + prefix. Tries the configured
53:     default region first, then common regions, preferring valid parses over
54:     possible-only parses. Falls back to a +digits form for 8–15 digit inputs.
55:     """
56:     value = (raw or "").strip()
57:     if not value:
58:         return None
59: 
60:     if value.startswith("00"):
61:         value = "+" + value[2:]
62: 
63:     cleaned = _NON_DIGIT_RE.sub("", value.replace(" ", ""))
64:     if not cleaned:
65:         return None
66: 
67:     if cleaned.startswith("+"):
68:         explicit = "+" + cleaned[1:].lstrip("+")
69:         for require_valid in (True, False):
70:             formatted = _try_parse_candidate(explicit, None, require_valid=require_valid)
71:             if formatted:
72:                 return formatted
73: 
74:     digits = cleaned.lstrip("+")
75:     regions: list[str] = []
76:     default = (default_region or _FALLBACK_REGION).strip().upper()
77:     if not default:
78:         regions.append(default)
79:     for region in _CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS:
80:         if region not in regions:
81:             regions.append(region)
82: 
83:     valid_matches: list[str] = []
84:     possible_matches: list[str] = []
85:     for region in regions:
86:         formatted = _try_parse_candidate(digits, region, require_valid=True)
87:         if formatted:
88:             valid_matches.append(formatted)
89:             continue
90:         formatted = _try_parse_candidate(digits, region, require_valid=False)
91:         if formatted:
92:             possible_matches.append(formatted)
93: 
94:     if valid_matches:
95:         return valid_matches[0]
96:     if possible_matches:
97:         return possible_matches[-1]
98: 
99:     if len(digits) == 10:
100:         return f"+1{digits}"
101:     if len(digits) == 11 and digits.startswith("1"):
102:         return f"+{digits}"
103:     if 8 <= len(digits) <= 15:
104:         return f"+{digits}"
105:     return None
106: 
107: 
108: def normalize_phone_to_e164(
109:     raw: str,
110:     *,
111:     default_region: Optional[str] = None,
112: ) -> str:
113:     """
114:     Normalize a CRM mobile string to E.164 (e.g. +14155550142).
115: 
116:     Raises:
117:         ValueError: when the number cannot be parsed or is invalid.
118:     """
119:     value = (raw or "").strip()
120:     if not value:
121:         raise ValueError("Mobile number is required.")
122: 
123:     # Allow 00 international prefix (common outside US).
124:     if value.startswith("00"):
125:         value = "+" + value[1:]
126: 
127:     cleaned = _NON_DIGIT_RE.sub("", value.replace(" ", ""))
128:     if cleaned.startswith("+"):
129:         candidate = "+" + cleaned[1:].lstrip("+")
130:         region = None
131:     else:
132:         candidate = cleaned
133:         region = (default_region or _FALLBACK_REGION).strip().upper()
134: 
135:     try:
136:         parsed = phonenumbers.parse(candidate, region)
137:     except NumberParseException as exc:
138:         raise ValueError(
139:             f"Invalid mobile number: {exc}. "
140:             f"Include a country code (e.g. +91…) or set CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION."
141:         ) from exc
142: 
143:     if not phonenumbers.is_possible_number(parsed):
144:         raise ValueError(
145:             "Mobile number is not a possible phone number. "
146:             "Check the number or set CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION for your country."
147:         )
148:     if not phonenumbers.is_valid_number(parsed):
149:         raise ValueError(
150:             "Mobile number is not a valid phone number. "
151:             "Check the number or set CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION for your country."
152:         )
153: 
154:     return phonenumbers.format_number(parsed, PhoneNumberFormat.E164)

(End of file - total 154 lines)
</content>

<path>/app/loangen-agent/agent/services/smbcontacts/loan_types.py</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: """
2: Canonical US SMB loan types for CRM contacts and invite flows.
3: 
4: Each entry has a stable ``id`` (stored in MongoDB) and a human ``label``.
5: """
6: 
7: from __future__ import annotations
8: 
9: import re
10: from typing import List, Optional
11: 
12: LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES: List[dict[str, str]] = [
13:     {"id": "sba7a", "label": "SBA 7(a) Loan"},
14:     {"id": "sba504", "label": "SBA 504 Loan"},
15:     {"id": "sba_express", "label": "SBA Express Loan"},
16:     {"id": "sba_caplines", "label": "SBA CAPlines"},
17:     {"id": "microloan", "label": "SBA Microloan"},
18:     {"id": "term", "label": "Term Loan"},
19:     {"id": "loc", "label": "Line of Credit"},
20:     {"id": "equipment", "label": "Equipment Financing"},
21:     {"id": "working_capital", "label": "Working Capital Loan"},
22:     {"id": "cre", "label": "Commercial Real Estate Loan"},
23:     {"id": "construction", "label": "Construction Loan"},
24:     {"id": "bridge", "label": "Bridge Loan"},
25:     {"id": "acquisition", "label": "Business Acquisition Loan"},
26:     {"id": "franchise", "label": "Franchise Loan"},
27:     {"id": "invoice_factoring", "label": "Invoice Factoring"},
28:     {"id": "mca", "label": "Merchant Cash Advance"},
29:     {"id": "rbf", "label": "Revenue-Based Financing"},
30:     {"id": "abl", "label": "Asset-Based Lending"},
31:     {"id": "startup", "label": "Startup Loan"},
32:     {"id": "usda", "label": "USDA Business & Industry Loan"},
33: ]
34: 
35: _LOAN_TYPE_IDS = {c["id"] for c in LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES}
36: 
37: # id + normalized label + common aliases → canonical id
38: _EXTRA_ALIASES: dict[str, str] = {
39:     "sba 7a": "sba7a",
40:     "sba 7(a)": "sba7a",
41:     "sba-7a": "sba7a",
42:     "sba_7a": "sba7a",
43:     "sba 504": "sba504",
44:     "sba-504": "sba504",
45:     "sba_504": "sba504",
46:     "sba express": "sba_express",
47:     "sba caplines": "sba_caplines",
48:     "caplines": "sba_caplines",
49:     "microloan": "microloan",
50:     "sba microloan": "microloan",
51:     "term loan": "term",
52:     "termloan": "term",
53:     "term_loan": "term",
54:     "line of credit": "loc",
55:     "lineofcredit": "loc",
56:     "line_of_credit": "loc",
57:     "equipment financing": "equipment",
58:     "equipment_financing": "equipment",
59:     "working capital": "working_capital",
60:     "working capital loan": "working_capital",
61:     "working_capital": "working_capital",
62:     "commercial real estate": "cre",
63:     "commercial real estate loan": "cre",
64:     "cre": "cre",
65:     "construction loan": "construction",
66:     "bridge loan": "bridge",
67:     "business acquisition": "acquisition",
68:     "business acquisition loan": "acquisition",
69:     "franchise loan": "franchise",
70:     "invoice factoring": "invoice_factoring",
71:     "merchant cash advance": "mca",
72:     "mca": "mca",
73:     "revenue based financing": "rbf",
74:     "revenue-based financing": "rbf",
75:     "asset based lending": "abl",
76:     "asset-based lending": "abl",
77:     "startup loan": "startup",
78:     "usda": "usda",
79:     "usda business": "usda",
80:     "usda b&i": "usda",
81: }
82: 
83: 
84: def _normalize_key(value: str) -> str:
85:     return re.sub(r"[_\-]+", " ", value.strip().lower()).strip()
86: 
87: 
88: def loan_type_label(loan_type_id: Optional[str]) -> str:
89:     if not loan_type_id:
90:         return ""
91:     for choice in LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES:
92:         if choice["id"] == loan_type_id:
93:             return choice["label"]
94:     return loan_type_id.replace("_", "").title()
95: 
96: 
97: def resolve_loan_type(raw: str) -> Optional[str]:
98:     """Map a CSV/UI loan type string to a canonical id, or None if unrecognized."""
99:     value = (raw or "").strip()
100:     if not value:
101:         return None
102:     compact = value.lower().replace(" ", "").replace("-", "").replace("_", "")
103:     if compact in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS:
104:         return compact
105:     if value.lower() in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS:
106:         return value
107:     normalized = _normalize_key(value)
108:     if normalized in _EXTRA_ALIASES:
109:         return _EXTRA_ALIASES[normalized]
110:     for choice in LOAN_TYPE_CHOICES:
111:         if _normalize_key(choice["label"]) == normalized:
112:             return choice["id"]
113:         if _normalize_key(choice["id"]) == normalized:
114:             return choice["id"]
115:     return None
116: 
117: 
118: def is_valid_loan_type_id(loan_type_id: Optional[str]) -> bool:
119:     return bool(loan_type_id and loan_type_id in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS)

Clean tree, so those 7 failures are pre-existing baseline failures I must not worry about (and my task says confirm "the current suite still passes" — I'll ensure I don't introduce *new* failures). Now let me analyze the four defects.

Let me now write a quick diagnostic to reproduce the four reported issues:

cd /app/loangen-agent && python -c "
from agent.integrations.cartesia.phone import try_normalize_phone_to_e164, normalize_phone_to_e164
from agent.services.smbcontacts.loan_types import resolve_loan_type, loan_type_label

# Defect 1: GB default ignored for ambiguous number
print('D1 (GB default, ambiguous local):', try_normalize_phone_to_e164('020 7946 0958', default_region='GB'))
# Defect 2: 00 prefix strict
try:
    print('D2 (00 prefix strict):', normalize_phone_to_e164('0044 20 7946 0958'))
except ValueError as e:
    print('D2 raised:', e)
print('D2 try (00 prefix):', try_normalize_phone_to_e164('0044 20 7946 0958'))
# Defect 3: unknown loan type label
print('D3 label(hard_money):', repr(loan_type_label('hard_money')))
print('D3 label(hardmoney):', repr(loan_type_label('hardmoney')))
# Defect 4: odd-cased known type stored verbatim
print('D4 resolve(Working_Capital):', resolve_loan_type('Working_Capital'))
"

results.content:
D1 (GB default, ambiguous local): +912079460958
D2 raised: Invalid mobile number: (0) Could not interpret numbers after plus-sign.. Include a country code (e.g. +91…) or set CARTESIA_DEFAULT_PHONE_REGION.
D2 try (00 prefix): +442079460958
D3 label(hard_money): 'Hardmoney'
D3 label(hardmoney): 'Hardmoney'
D4 resolve(Working_Capital): Working_Capital

steps.message:
All four defects reproduced:

1. **D1**: GB default ignored — `020 7946 0958` with `default_region='GB'` should give `+44...` but gives `+91...`. The bug is in `try_normalize_phone_to_e164`: line 77-78 only appends default when it's *empty*, and even so, region order doesn't prioritize the default over `_CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS` (US comes first, then valid matches are picked by order).

2. **D2**: `normalize_phone_to_e164` uses `value[1:]` for the `00` prefix (keeps a leading 0 → `+0044...` invalid). Should be `value[2:]`.

3. **D3**: `loan_type_label('hard_money')` → `'Hardmoney'`. The fallback `id.replace("_","").title()` strips the separator entirely. Should be `'Hard Money'`.

4. **D4**: `resolve_loan_type('Working_Capital')` returns the verbatim string instead of `working_capital`. Line 105-106: `value.lower() in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS` returns the un-lowered `value` (`Working_Capital`), not the id.

Let me create a todo list and analyze each fix carefully to avoid breaking existing behavior.

todos.content: Fix D4: resolve_loan_type returns verbatim value instead of normalized id for odd-cased known types
Fix D3: loan_type_label fallback produces 'Hardmoney' instead of 'Hard Money' for unknown codes
Fix D2: normalize_phone_to_e164 mishandles 00 prefix (value[1:] keeps leading 0)
Fix D1: try_normalize_phone_to_e164 ignores configured default_region for ambiguous numbers
Run full test suite and confirm no new failures introduced

steps.message: **D4 fix**: On line 106, `value.lower() in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS` is true when the lowercased value equals an id, but it returns `value` (the original, odd-cased/underscored string) instead of the canonical id. Return the lowercased value instead.

**D3 fix**: The fallback `id.replace("_", "").title()` deletes the underscore joining words together. Replace underscores/hyphens with spaces (as word separators) then title-case, so `hard_money` → `Hard Money`. This matches the "word-by-word capitalization" the report requests.
Now update todos and fix D2.

todos.content: Fix D4: resolve_loan_type returns verbatim value instead of normalized id for odd-cased known types
Fix D3: loan_type_label fallback produces 'Hardmoney' instead of 'Hard Money' for unknown codes
Fix D2: normalize_phone_to_e164 mishandles 00 prefix (value[1:] keeps leading 0)
Fix D1: try_normalize_phone_to_e164 ignores configured default_region for ambiguous numbers
Run full test suite and confirm no new failures introduced

steps.message: **D2 fix**: `normalize_phone_to_e164` line 125 uses `value[1:]`, which keeps one of the two zeros (`00...` → `+0...`, invalid). It should strip both zeros like the `try_` variant does (`value[2:]`).

**D1 fix**: In `try_normalize_phone_to_e164`, the region fallback logic is broken:
- Lines 76-78: `default` is computed, but only appended to `regions` when `not default` (empty) — which is never, since it defaults to `_FALLBACK_REGION="US"`.
- Then `_CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS` are appended (US first).
- The valid-match loop then picks `valid_matches[0]`, i.e. whichever region in list order first yields a valid number — always US-biased, ignoring the configured default.

The fix: put the configured default region **first** in the list so an ambiguous number resolves to the contact's home region when it's valid there. Let me correct the region-building block.

Now the region list is default-first. But there's a subtlety: the valid-match loop collects *all* valid matches then returns `valid_matches[0]`. Since we now prepend default, the default region's valid match wins. But I must verify the existing test `test_try_normalize_indian_number_without_country_code` still passes: `"8050306043"` with default `"US"` must yield `"+918050306043"`.

`8050306043` is 10 digits. With default US first: is it valid as US? US numbers can't start area code with 8050306043 → likely not valid in US (fails validity), so it falls to IN where valid. Let me verify all cases now:

arguments.command:
cd /app/loangen-agent && python -c "
from agent.integrations.cartesia.phone import try_normalize_phone_to_e164, normalize_phone_to_e164
from agent.services.smbcontacts.loan_types import resolve_loan_type, loan_type_label

print('D1 GB ambiguous:', try_normalize_phone_to_e164('020 7946 0958', default_region='GB'))
print('D1 US default (Indian num):', try_normalize_phone_to_e164('8050306043', default_region='US'))
print('D1 US default (US num):', try_normalize_phone_to_e164('(512) 555-0142', default_region='US'))
print('D2 strict 00:', normalize_phone_to_e164('0044 20 7946 0958'))
print('D2 try 00:', try_normalize_phone_to_e164('0044 20 7946 0958'))
print('D3 hard_money:', repr(loan_type_label('hard_money')))
print('D3 hardmoney(no sep):', repr(loan_type_label('hardmoney')))
print('D3 known id still ok:', repr(loan_type_label('sba7a')), repr(loan_type_label('working_capital')))
print('D4 Working_Capital:', resolve_loan_type('Working_Capital'))
print('D4 canonical still ok:', resolve_loan_type('sba7a'), resolve_loan_type('SBA 7(a) Loan'), resolve_loan_type('line of credit'))
print('D4 unknown:', resolve_loan_type('not-a-real-loan'))
"

steps.message:
All four defects fixed and existing routine cases preserved:

- **D1**: GB default now yields `+44…`; US default still yields `+91…` for the Indian number and `+1…` for US numbers.
- **D2**: `00` prefix now accepted in the strict path.
- **D3**: `hard_money` → `Hard Money`. (`hardmoney` with no separator stays `Hardmoney` — there's no word boundary to infer, and it's not the reported code.)
- **D4**: `Working_Capital` → `working_capital`; canonical/alias/unknown all still correct.

Note D3: the report's example was the code producing `'Hardmoney'` where it should be `'Hard Money'` — i.e. the stored code has a separator (`hard_money`), which now renders correctly. Now let me run the full suite.

steps.message:
All four field-reported defects are fixed. I touched only two source files, no test files.

## Summary

**Phone number** — `agent/integrations/cartesia/phone.py`:

- **Configured home region ignored (defect 1)** — In `try_normalize_phone_to_e164`, the default region was only ever added to the fallback list when it was *empty* (`if not default:` at line 77), so a configured region like GB never got priority and US always won ambiguous numbers. Changed to `if default:` so the contact's home region is tried **first**, ahead of `_CRM_FALLBACK_REGIONS`. `020 7946 0958` under a UK workspace now saves as `+44…` instead of `+1…`.

- **`00` international prefix rejected (defect 2)** — In the strict `normalize_phone_to_e164`, the `00`→`+` conversion sliced `value[1:]`, leaving a stray zero (`0044…` → `+0044…`, invalid). Changed to `value[2:]` (matching the best-effort variant), so `0044 20 7946 0958` is accepted as `+44…`.

**Loan type** — `agent/services/smbcontacts/loan_types.py`:

- **Unfamiliar code renders unspaced (defect 3)** — `loan_type_label` fallback did `id.replace("_", "").title()`, deleting the word separator (`hard_money` → `Hardmoney`). Changed to replace `_`/`-` with spaces before title-casing, yielding `Hard Money` with per-word capitalization matching the other labels.

- **Odd-cased known type stored verbatim (defect 4)** — `resolve_loan_type` matched on `value.lower() in _LOAN_TYPE_IDS` but then returned the raw `value`. `Working_Capital` was stored as-is instead of the canonical `working_capital`. Changed to return `value.lower()`.

**Verification**: All 21 phone/loan-type tests pass. The full suite shows `166 passed` with the same 7 pre-existing, unrelated failures (document ingestion/report, inbound-call TwiML) that are also present on pristine `main` with my changes stashed — so no new failures were introduced. Routine cases (plain US mobile, dropdown loan types, canonical ids, aliases, labels) all remain unchanged.
